name: Yesterday-today-and-tomorrow
description: Yesterday-Today-and-Tomorrow (Brunfelsia pauciflora), named for its flowers that change colour over three days, is moderately toxic to cats. All parts of the plant contain brunfelsamidine and other steroidal alkaloids. It is a member of the nightshade family and is particularly known for causing neurological symptoms.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: brunfelsamidine and related steroidal alkaloids; all parts of the plant are toxic
Mechanism: alkaloids affect the central nervous system, causing excitation and then CNS depression
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, muscle tremors, seizures, hyperthermia; characteristically produces a syndrome that alternates agitation with sedation
Frequently used as a decorative garden shrub in tropical regions — keep cats away from the garden
Seek veterinary care promptly if ingestion is suspected; the neurological syndrome can escalate rapidly

symptoms.name: Tachycardia (rapid heart rate)
symptoms.notes: This is caused by the plant's toxic alkaloids (atropine, scopolamine, and hyoscyamine)1. In cases where the cardiovascular system becomes severely depressed, the veterinarian may need to administer cardiac stimulants1. 2. Dry Mouth

symptoms.name: Dry mouth
symptoms.notes: Caused by the ingestion of the flowers, leaves, bark, or roots1. 3. Dilated Pupils

symptoms.name: Dilated pupils
symptoms.notes: A direct systemic response to the alkaloid toxins1. 4. Loss of Coordination and Tremors

symptoms.name: Ataxia (loss of coordination) and tremors
symptoms.notes: These symptoms highlight the severe neurological disruption caused by the plant's toxic compounds1. 5. Urinary Retention

symptoms.name: Urinary retention
symptoms.notes: Occurs alongside other systemic signs of poisoning after the animal consumes the plant material1. 6. Depression, Deep Sedation, or Coma

symptoms.name: Depression, deep sedation, and sometimes coma
symptoms.notes: While poisoning from this plant can lead to a profound comatose state requiring respiratory stimulants and supportive treatment, it is notably not reported to cause death
